title: Challenge Lab - Resolve Connectivity Issues Between VPC, EC2 Instances, and Load Balancer

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12-digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your Username and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed in to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign in to AWS Portal

XYZ Company wants to deploy a publicly accessible web application. As a cloud engineer, your objective is to manually set up a VPC with public subnets, launch an EC2 instance with Apache installed, configure internet access using an Internet Gateway and route table, and create an Application Load Balancer to distribute traffic. You will validate access using the ALB DNS and EC2 IP, and perform basic troubleshooting if needed.
Create a VPC Region: us-east-1 Create VPC Name: MyVPC IPv4 CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16 IPv6: No IPv6 CIDR block Tenancy: Default
Region: us-east-1
Create VPC Name: MyVPC IPv4 CIDR: 10.0.0.0/16 IPv6: No IPv6 CIDR block Tenancy: Default
Name: MyVPC
IPv6: No IPv6 CIDR block
Tenancy: Default
Create Public Subnets Subnet 1 AZ: us-east-1a CIDR: 10.0.1.0/24 Subnet 2 AZ: us-east-1b CIDR: 10.0.2.0/24
Subnet 1 AZ: us-east-1a CIDR: 10.0.1.0/24
AZ: us-east-1a
Subnet 2 AZ: us-east-1b CIDR: 10.0.2.0/24
AZ: us-east-1b
Create and Attach Internet Gateway
Create Public Route Table Create Add Route: Destination: 0.0.0.0/0 Target: MyInternetGateway Associate both public subnets with this route table
Create
Add Route: Destination: 0.0.0.0/0 Target: MyInternetGateway
Destination: 0.0.0.0/0
Target: MyInternetGateway
Associate both public subnets with this route table

Launch EC2 Instance Name: EC2server AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel Instance Type: t2.micro Network: MyVPC, Subnet: MyPublicSubnet1 Auto-assign Public IP: Enabled Security Group: EC2server-SG Inbound rules: Allow HTTP and optionally SSH User data : Install Apache and create index.html with response message
Name: EC2server
AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 kernel
Instance Type: t2.micro
Network: MyVPC, Subnet: MyPublicSubnet1
Auto-assign Public IP: Enabled
Security Group: EC2server-SG Inbound rules: Allow HTTP and optionally SSH
Inbound rules: Allow HTTP and optionally SSH
User data : Install Apache and create index.html with response message

Create Application Load Balancer Name: Myapplication-LB Scheme: Internet-facing IP Type: IPv4 VPC: MyVPC Subnets: Select both public subnets Security Group: EC2server-SG Listener: HTTP on port 80 Target Group: Name: Apache-TG Type: Instance Add EC2server to targets
Name: Myapplication-LB
Scheme: Internet-facing
IP Type: IPv4
VPC: MyVPC
Subnets: Select both public subnets
Security Group: EC2server-SG
Listener: HTTP on port 80
Target Group: Name: Apache-TG Type: Instance Add EC2server to targets
Name: Apache-TG
Type: Instance
Add EC2server to targets
Test Load Balancer Copy DNS name from ALB and open it in browser.
Copy DNS name from ALB and open it in browser.
Troubleshooting (if needed) Attach Internet Gateway to VPC Associate public subnets with route table Allow HTTP (port 80) in security group Ensure EC2 has a public IP assigned
Attach Internet Gateway to VPC
Associate public subnets with route table
Allow HTTP (port 80) in security group
Ensure EC2 has a public IP assigned
Reboot EC2 Go to EC2 > Select instance > Actions > Reboot
Go to EC2 > Select instance > Actions > Reboot
Test EC2 Public IP Copy public IPv4 address of EC2 and open in browser
Copy public IPv4 address of EC2 and open in browser
SSH into EC2 (optional) Use EC2 Instance Connect from AWS Console
Use EC2 Instance Connect from AWS Console

Reinstall Apache (Optional Troubleshooting) Switch to root, update system, install & start Apache, enable service, check status. Add content to index.html, restart Apache, verify via public IP or Load Balancer DNS.
Switch to root, update system, install & start Apache, enable service, check status.
Add content to index.html, restart Apache, verify via public IP or Load Balancer DNS.
Click on the Validate to complete the challenge.
Sign out of the AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from the lab console.
